title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
At the town hall in Aroo, Ellery Queen discovers that the telegram he found was never delivered to Sheriff Redden because the sheriff and Mayor Matt Hollis left for a week-long fishing trip on the Ohio River; the office caretaker had simply signed and shelved it. Driven by fear, Ellery drives his Duesenberg to a remote location, armed with a loaded semi-automatic pistol. He follows a path to a clearing where he finds footprints in the damp earth and a building with a door slightly ajar. Upon entering, he encounters a "terrible sight" and closes the heavy door behind him.

Returning to the town hall, Ellery meets a doorman who gives him a note left by a tall, gangly man described as looking like "old Abe Lincoln." Ellery realizes the note was actually left by Professor Yadley, who traveled to the area by private plane to find Vane and Isam. In the note, Yadley reports seeing a limping man, presumably Croscak, who rented a car at Yellow Bay and traveled to a hotel in Stowellville, Ohio. Yadley instructs Ellery to go to Stowellville immediately. Ellery instructs the doorman to deliver the note to incoming officials from New York, specifically Eames and Vance, and then speeds away.

Meanwhile, in Bradwood, Officer Wain and District Attorney Esam arrive with a representative from the U.S. Attorney's office, escorting Percy and Elizabeth Rein. Following a report from Professor Adler, the officials realize they must pursue the suspects.
